facet: cgroup per-tenant group accounting + isolation — can a co-op meter and isolate tenants on shared relay hardware?
topology: node-1 (fat, 4c/15G). Two relay 'tenants' each a transient systemd service with its own cgroup (CPUAccounting+MemoryAccounting): relayA.service (https 3343/quic 3478), relayB.service (https 3353/quic 3488). Responder + generator co-located per tenant (relay-only passthrough). Generators co-located => absolute throughput understated (known E0/E1 caveat); per-slice ACCOUNTING fidelity is the measured quantity.
method: RUN1 accounting: asymmetric load (tenantA 40 conns x 32MiB = 1.28 GiB; tenantB 4 conns x 1MiB = 4 MiB), both CPUQuota=300%; read each service cgroup cpu.stat usage_usec delta + memory.current. RUN2 isolation: set relayA CPUQuota=50%, drive BOTH tenants 30 conns x 32MiB; measure cores used per slice = cpu_usec_delta / wall.

run1_accounting.finding: Per-slice cgroup accounting attributes CPU to the right tenant with high fidelity — the heavy tenant's slice accrued ~249x the CPU-seconds of the light tenant, tracking the ~320x byte asymmetry (the gap is fixed per-connection handshake cost on the light tenant). RSS is also per-slice. A cooperative gets each tenant's bill for free from the cgroup, no app-level metering.

run2_isolation.finding: Under identical 30 x 32MiB load, relayA's CPUQuota=50% held it to 0.49 cores (cap honored to 2 decimals) while relayB ran uncapped at 0.51 cores. The cap throttled CPU without stranding connections — tenantA still established 30/30. A noisy tenant is isolated, not allowed to monopolize the box.

result.exit: MET — per-slice CPU/RSS accounting is accurate under mixed load (249:1 bill), and a CPU cap isolates a tenant without breaking its connectivity. A co-op can both meter and contain tenants on shared relay hardware using only cgroup primitives.
result.decision: Splintered-process-per-tenant (E5) is justified for ACCOUNTING + ISOLATION (not CPU utilization — E1 showed one fat process already scales across cores). The two rationales are orthogonal: E1 = use all cores with one process; E5 = meter/isolate with several.
result.caveat: Co-located generators were the throughput bottleneck (establish_ms 176-316s), so absolute relay capacity is understated; the measured quantity here is accounting/isolation fidelity, which co-location does not distort. Memory cap (MemoryMax) isolation was not stress-tested to OOM; only CPU cap was driven to its limit.
